title: Krishna Steals the Butter
referenceImagePrompt: A warm, hand-illustrated children's storybook portrait of young Krishna, blue skin, playful smile, peacock feather in dark hair, yellow dhoti, round face with big expressive eyes, soft painterly art style, warm golden lighting, front-facing, simple village background, high detail, consistent character design sheet

scenes.narration: Long ago, in the little village of Gokul, there lived a mischievous boy named Krishna. He loved only one thing more than his friends - fresh, creamy butter.
scenes.motionPrompt: Krishna walks playfully through a sunny village lane, looking around curiously, gentle camera pan, soft breeze moving his hair

scenes.narration: One morning, Krishna crept quietly into a neighbor's kitchen, climbed onto a wooden stool, and reached into a clay pot of fresh butter.
scenes.motionPrompt: Krishna tiptoes into a rustic kitchen, climbs a small wooden stool, reaches up toward a clay pot on a high shelf, mischievous expression

scenes.narration: But Krishna did not eat the butter alone. He shared it with his friends and even left some for the poor children of the village who had none.
scenes.motionPrompt: Krishna sits with a group of smiling children, happily sharing butter from the pot, warm and joyful atmosphere, soft golden light

scenes.narration: This story comes from the Bhagavata Purana, Book 10, Chapter 8. It teaches us that Krishna's butter stealing was never about greed - it was about sharing.
scenes.motionPrompt: Krishna sits peacefully under a tree holding a flute, looking gently toward the viewer, calm and warm expression, soft ambient light

scenes.narration: So the next time you have something you love, remember Krishna - the greatest joy comes from sharing it with others.
scenes.motionPrompt: Krishna smiles warmly and waves toward the viewer, peacock feather gently swaying, soft zoom in, heartwarming close
